title: chargeback — published events
description: Every event implements `ShouldDispatchAfterCommit` so listeners never fire against a rolled-back write. Three P0 events: `ChargebackFiled` (money forcibly reversed by bank — P0 admin signal); `ChargebackLost` (P1 signal — the finalization chain fires TWO offsetting transactions + invoice state update + clawback fan-out + marketing negative-value-conversion + fraud signal), `ChargebackRateThresholdExceeded` (P1 signal — tenant's chargeback rate crossed VDMP/MDMP threshold, merchant-account termination risk). Financial events (`ChargebackLost` / `ChargebackClawbackReferral` / `ChargebackClawbackCoupon`) additionally fan out to finance::invoice for state update + finance::transaction for double-entry offset + finance::credit-note (via invoice module) for reversal document. `ChargebackLost` fires a marketing negative-value conversion downstream to protect ROAS + retrain fraud models on ad networks — MORE HEAVILY than refunds because chargebacks are a fraud correlation signal.

events.name: ChargebackFiled
events.trigger: ChargebackRepository finished a successful create. Fires exactly once per chargeback. P0 signal — money has been forcibly reversed by the bank. Distinct from a customer-initiated PaymentDispute (which fires payment::PaymentDisputeFiled) — this fires when the bank ESCALATES to a full chargeback.

payload.payment_dispute_id: ULID (pdi_*) | null (null when originated directly via webhook, not escalated from a dispute)

events.name: ChargebackEvidenceRequested
events.trigger: The bank has requested evidence — this fires alongside ChargebackFiled (initial request) OR later when the bank requests ADDITIONAL evidence after reviewing an initial submission (CE3.0 second pass). Sets evidence_due_by on the chargeback.

payload.additional_evidence_requested: boolean (false on initial request; true on CE3.0 second pass)
payload.network_request_notes: string | null (bank's rationale for additional evidence)

events.name: ChargebackEvidenceSubmitted
events.trigger: ChargebackEvidenceRepository finished a successful create. Fires per submission attempt. Distinct from ChargebackFiled — this fires each time the merchant submits (or resubmits) evidence.

payload.evidence_shape_completeness_percent: integer 0-100 (how many of the 8 evidence fields are populated)

events.name: ChargebackUnderReview
events.trigger: Chargeback.status transitioned evidence_submitted -> under_bank_review. The bank confirmed receipt of evidence + started internal review. Typical wait 30-45 days.

events.name: ChargebackWon
events.trigger: Chargeback.status transitioned under_bank_review -> won. Bank sided with the merchant. Money returned. Note: won chargebacks still incur the initial network fee — banks don't refund the fee they collected. P1 event.

events.name: ChargebackLost
events.trigger: Chargeback.status transitioned under_bank_review -> lost. Bank sided with the customer. Money permanently gone. FIRES THE FINALIZATION CHAIN: offsetting transaction (disputed amount) + network fee transaction + invoice state update + clawback fan-out + marketing negative-conversion + fraud signal. P1 event.
consumers: marketing::FireMarketingChargebackFiledOnChargebackLost (negative-value conversion + fraud signal — ad-network ROAS adjustment + fraud model retraining)
observability::MetricsCollector (academorix.chargeback.lost_total labelled by network + reason_category + reason_code)

events.name: ChargebackExpired
events.trigger: Chargeback.status transitioned pending_evidence -> expired. Evidence window passed with no submission. Treated as an auto-loss — the finalization chain fires with the same steps as ChargebackLost.
consumers: chargeback::FinalizeChargebackJob (invokes the LOST finalization chain — auto-loss)
notifications::DispatchChargebackLostNotification (P1 admin — 'auto-lost due to missed evidence deadline')

events.name: ChargebackAcceptedRefund
events.trigger: Chargeback.status transitioned pending_evidence -> accepted_refund via RDR. RdrHandler created a Refund via refund::RefundOrchestrator + closed the pre-chargeback state. Terminal — cheapest defense (no network fee, no rate impact).

payload.rdr_threshold_cents: integer (the tenant's configured threshold at accept time)

events.name: ChargebackEvidenceOverdue
events.trigger: NotifyChargebackEvidenceOverdueJob detected a chargeback with evidence_due_by approaching (7 / 3 / 1 day cadence). Actionable — deadline missed = auto-loss. Not fired on the deadline itself; the transition to expired handles that separately via MarkExpiredChargebacksJob.

events.name: ChargebackClawbackReferral
events.trigger: ChargebackLost fired + ClawbackCoordinator detected that the disputed invoice was linked to a materialized ReferralReward. Cascades to growth::referrals for reward reversal.
consumers: referrals::ProcessReferralClawbackOnChargeback (Wave 5 — reverses the reward + notifies the referrer with a 'chargeback loss' reason)

events.name: ChargebackClawbackCoupon
events.trigger: ChargebackLost fired + ClawbackCoordinator detected that the disputed invoice had a redeemed coupon. Cascades to coupon module for redemption reversal.

events.name: ChargebackNetworkFeeRecorded
events.trigger: FinalizeChargebackJob step 2 completed — the network fee ($15-25 typical) recorded as a secondary offsetting Transaction. Fires alongside ChargebackTransactionRecorded (step 1) but for the network fee entry specifically.

events.name: ChargebackTransactionRecorded
events.trigger: FinalizeChargebackJob step 1 completed — the disputed amount recorded as an offsetting Transaction (debit chargebacks_expense, credit cash).

events.name: ChargebackWebhookReceived
events.trigger: payment::PaymentWebhookHandler dispatched a chargeback-typed webhook event. This event fires FIRST (before the chargeback is created or reconciled) to record the webhook receipt itself. HandleChargebackWebhook listener consumes it + calls ChargebackOrchestrator::createFromWebhook / ChargebackReconciler::reconcileFromWebhook.
consumers: chargeback::HandleChargebackWebhook (creates or reconciles the local chargeback)

events.name: ChargebackReconciled
events.trigger: Provider webhook OR CheckChargebackStatusJob detected a state change on the provider side. The chargeback's status is now aligned with the provider's authoritative record. Distinct from ChargebackWon / ChargebackLost — this event fires FIRST (before terminal transition) to record the reconciliation itself. drift_detected=true when the local status differed from provider.

payload.drift_detected: boolean (true when the local status differed from provider)

events.name: ChargebackRateThresholdExceeded
events.trigger: MonitorChargebackRateJob detected the tenant's rolling 60-day chargeback rate crossed the network's VDMP/MDMP threshold (0.9% Visa / 1.5% Mastercard / 1.0% Amex / 1.0% Discover). P1 signal — merchant-account termination risk if sustained. Actionable — ops must intervene.

payload.days_over_threshold: integer (how long the rate has been over threshold)
